mcqs.question: In the Producer-Consumer problem using a bounded buffer of size N, what is the maximum number of items that can be in the buffer at once?
mcqs.explanation: A bounded buffer of size N can hold at most N items at any time. This constraint is enforced using counting semaphores to track empty and filled slots.

mcqs.question: In the Readers-Writers problem, what is the maximum number of readers that can read simultaneously?

options.text: N (where N is number of readers)

mcqs.explanation: The readers-writers problem allows multiple readers to access the shared data simultaneously because reading does not modify the data. Any number of readers can read at the same time.

mcqs.question: Which classical problem demonstrates deadlock most clearly?
mcqs.explanation: The Dining Philosophers problem is the classic example of deadlock - if each philosopher picks up their left fork simultaneously, all wait forever for the right fork.

mcqs.question: In the Sleeping Barber problem, what happens when a customer arrives and the barber is sleeping?

options.text: Customer wakes up the barber and gets served

mcqs.explanation: The sleeping barber problem models producer-consumer synchronization. When a customer arrives and finds the barber sleeping, the customer wakes the barber and gets serviced immediately.

mcqs.question: What synchronization primitive is most commonly used to solve the Producer-Consumer problem?
mcqs.explanation: Semaphores (specifically counting semaphores) are commonly used - one for empty slots and one for filled slots, both initialized to appropriate values.

mcqs.question: In the Dining Philosophers problem, if philosopher i picks up the left fork first, which fork should they pick up next to avoid deadlock?

options.text: Wait until right fork is available

mcqs.explanation: One solution is to have philosophers pick up the lower-numbered fork first, or have one philosopher pick forks in opposite order to break symmetry and prevent circular wait deadlock.

mcqs.question: What is the reader priority solution problem in Readers-Writers?
mcqs.explanation: In reader priority solutions, writers may starve because new readers keep arriving and get served first, causing writers to wait indefinitely.

mcqs.question: In a bounded buffer implementation, what does the 'in' pointer represent?
mcqs.explanation: The 'in' pointer tracks the next position where the producer will insert a new item. The 'out' pointer tracks where the consumer will remove the next item.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a classical synchronization problem?
mcqs.explanation: Banker's Algorithm is a deadlock avoidance algorithm, not a classical synchronization problem. The others are all classic process coordination problems.

mcqs.question: In the Cigarette Smokers problem, what triggers a smoker to roll a cigarette?

options.text: Agent places two ingredients smoker needs
Any two ingredients appear on table

mcqs.explanation: Each smoker needs the ingredient they don't have. The agent places two ingredients on the table, and the smoker who has the third ingredient takes all ingredients and smokes.

mcqs.question: What happens in the bounded buffer when empty = 0 and a consumer tries to consume?

options.text: Consumer blocks (waits) until producer adds an item

mcqs.explanation: The consumer must wait (block) when the buffer is empty. The empty semaphore tracks available filled slots, and when it is zero, the consumer cannot proceed.

mcqs.question: In the first readers-writers solution, what protects the readcount variable?
mcqs.explanation: The readcount variable (tracking number of active readers) is protected by a mutex or binary semaphore to ensure atomic access when incrementing/decrementing.

mcqs.question: What is the minimum number of chairs in the waiting room for the Sleeping Barber problem to avoid customer loss?

options.text: Equal to number of potential customers

mcqs.explanation: The waiting room should have enough chairs for all potential customers to wait. If all chairs are full, arriving customers leave (representing lost requests in real systems).

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the bounded buffer operation (enqueue/dequeue)?
mcqs.explanation: Both enqueue (producer) and dequeue (consumer) operations on a circular buffer are O(1) - constant time operations as long as slots are available.

mcqs.question: A solution to the Dining Philosophers using semaphores may suffer from which problem?
mcqs.explanation: A simple semaphore solution can deadlock (all philosophers pick left fork simultaneously) and individual philosophers can starve if they never get both forks.

mcqs.question: In the Producer-Consumer problem, what is the purpose of the mutex semaphore?

options.text: Ensure mutual exclusion for buffer access

mcqs.explanation: The mutex semaphore provides mutual exclusion - only one producer or consumer can access the buffer at a time to prevent race conditions on the buffer itself.

mcqs.question: What is the 'remainder section' in process synchronization?

options.text: The code executing outside critical section

mcqs.explanation: The remainder section is the part of the process code that executes outside the critical section, where the process makes no requests for entry to its critical section.

mcqs.question: Which solution approach can completely prevent deadlock in Dining Philosophers?

options.text: Having odd-numbered philosophers pick left first, even pick right first
Allowing philosophers to take forks anytime

mcqs.explanation: By breaking the symmetry - making one philosopher pick forks in opposite order - circular wait condition is broken, preventing deadlock completely.

mcqs.question: In producer-consumer with N buffers, initial values of empty and full semaphores should be:
mcqs.explanation: empty=N (all N slots are empty initially) and full=0 (no items produced yet). The producer waits on empty (decrements) and signals full; consumer does opposite.

mcqs.question: What does the rw_mutex semaphore do in readers-writers solution?

options.text: Controls both reader and writer access to shared data

mcqs.explanation: The rw_mutex semaphore controls exclusive access to the shared data. Writers must acquire it exclusively; readers acquire it in shared mode (multiple readers can hold it together).
